mnemonics.title: Parse Tree Structure
mnemonics.content: ROOT to LEAVES: Start Symbol flows downward - remember R→L flow like 'Rain Leaves' but backwards: Leaves anchor the structure, Root rules all.

mnemonics.title: Tree Traversals
mnemonics.content: L-Root-Right (Infix) = Inorder = 'In the middle' | Root-L-Right (Prefix) = Preorder = 'Precede operands' | Left-Right-Root (Postfix) = Postorder = 'Post means after, last'

mnemonics.title: Parse Tree Nodes
mnemonics.content: INside = INternal = Non-terminals | LEaf = LEft = Terminals - Think 'Leaves End with Letters (Terminals)'

acronyms.usage: Another name for parse tree showing complete grammatical structure
Simplified tree for semantic analysis, removing redundant syntax
Postfix notation produced by postorder traversal of parse tree

visualTips.visualization: Draw tree top-down with root at top, branches spreading downward, leaves at bottom forming a horizontal line (the yield)
Think of derivation as top-down breakdown: start symbol splits into branches following production rules until only terminals remain
Multiple trees for same string - picture a single input connecting to two different tree structures like a Y junction

analogies.analogy: Like a family tree showing ancestry - root ancestor at top, generations below, leaf descendants at bottom
Parse tree is complete blueprint with every nail and beam; AST is architect's simplified sketch showing only essential structure
Terminals are 'finished products' (leaves); Non-terminals are 'work in progress' (components that need further expansion)
